rationale: Step 4 diagnosis showed best method (ODE) at 66.3%, below the 80% target. The dominant failure signature on the ODE method was ratio<=0.02 on tests where expected=increased (central-metabolism KOs and OE tests), plus flat ratio=1.0 on environmental-source tests. Inspection of the Lycopene_Titer equation revealed that FPP and GPP are wired as INHIBITORS of Lycopene_Titer (edges N077, N078), carrying a legitimate DOI (Cheng 2022) for 'toxic accumulation at very high MVA OE'. However, the FLASH-P framework applies inhibition MONOTONICALLY — at any FPP > 1, lycopene drops — whereas the Cheng 2022 claim is a THRESHOLD phenomenon that only manifests at extremely high flux. This monotonic mis-application inverts the sign for every perturbation that raises FPP (IspB KD, IDI OE, DXS OE cascade), producing many of the ODE failures. The Ma 2020 review (10.3390/molecules25143136) confirms the canonical FPP -> GGPP (CrtE) -> Phytoene (CrtB) -> Lycopene (CrtI) cascade; FPP and GPP are precursors, not direct inhibitors. Removing N077 and N078 restores the canonical cascade interpretation under the algebraic/ODE framework. The Cheng 2022 toxicity is accepted as a framework limitation (the algebraic/ODE formulation cannot represent threshold-based toxicity). Encoding fix for T097 (Gnd vs GND case mismatch) recovers one previously skipped test.

fixes.reason: T007 (IspB KD, alg ratio=0.015, expected=increased) and OE tests across the MEP/MVA arms are inverted by this edge. FPP is the immediate precursor to GGPP -> Phytoene -> Lycopene via CrtE/B/I; it activates lycopene via the cascade, not inhibits it. The Cheng 2022 toxicity DOI is valid biology but is a threshold phenomenon the monotone algebraic/ODE framework cannot capture.
fixes.biological_justification: Ma 2020 canonical MEP -> FPP -> GGPP (CrtE) -> Phytoene (CrtB) -> Lycopene (CrtI) cascade in E. coli. FPP is a substrate of CrtE, not an inhibitor of lycopene. Framework-scope note: Cheng 2022 toxicity only manifests at extreme MVA midstream over-expression; the monotone bounded-inverse inhibition in FLASH-P predicts decreased lycopene for any FPP > baseline, which is incorrect for all normal-expression tests. Framework limitation acknowledged.
fixes.evidence_title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene (Ma 2020)

fixes.reason: GPP (C10 geranyl diphosphate) is an enzyme-bound intermediate of IspA that is processed directly to FPP by the same enzyme in E. coli strains without monoterpene pathway enzymes. Modeling it as a parallel sink for lycopene is incorrect in standard lycopene-producing strains. The Cheng 2022 threshold-toxicity claim applies jointly to GPP+FPP at extreme flux; same framework limitation as N078.
fixes.biological_justification: IspA is a sequential prenyltransferase (DMAPP + IPP -> GPP -> FPP) — GPP is transferred between active-site cycles, not released as a free regulatory metabolite. No monoterpene synthase is present in the baseline lycopene strain, so GPP does not accumulate.
fixes.evidence_title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene (Ma 2020)

fixes.description: Map 'Gnd' to the existing GND node (case-insensitive reconciliation miss).
fixes.reason: The Step 3 reconciler flagged T097 (Gnd KO) as not_in_network, but GND (6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase) IS a source node in the network with GND -> NADPH (+1). Setting GND gene_modifier=0.0 on T097 drops NADPH, which feeds CrtI activation — predicting decreased lycopene, matching expected=decreased.
fixes.biological_justification: Standard biochemistry: 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase (Gnd) is the second dehydrogenase of the oxidative pentose phosphate pathway and a major NADPH source. Loss of Gnd reduces NADPH available for CrtI-mediated phytoene desaturation.

updates.reconciliation_note: Gnd -> GND (case fix, applied iteration 1 refinement)
